general_provision.override: No waiver of disclaimer of reliance — no statement/questionnaire/acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements

overrides.override_summary: Must comply with Fast Food Act (Cal. Lab. Code §§1474-1475) — health, safety, employment standards including minimum wages, working hours, working conditions
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional labor cost burden not reflected in Item 7 estimates; franchisee solely responsible for compliance

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver/anti-reliance provision supersedes any document executed in connection with franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects California franchisees from releasing claims under state franchise law

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver/anti-reliance provision
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard state franchise law protection

overrides.override_summary: Any release at renewal/transfer will not apply to claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee retains right to sue under Maryland law even after signing general release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects bankrupt franchisees from automatic termination

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims under Maryland Franchise Law, unless preempted by federal law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta, Georgia venue requirement for Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims under Maryland Franchise Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Georgia choice of law for Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland Franchise Law must be brought within 3 years after franchise grant
overrides.why_it_matters: Establishes specific limitations period for Maryland claims

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate prior to expiration except for good cause; franchisee gets written notice and reasonable opportunity to cure (up to 30 days)
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan law prohibits termination without good cause and mandatory cure period

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse renewal without fairly compensating franchisee for FMV of inventory/supplies/equipment/fixtures (if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from operating under other brand or <6 months notice)
overrides.why_it_matters: Requires buyback at FMV upon non-renewal under certain conditions

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require arbitration or litigation outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta venue; franchisee can litigate in Michigan

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer except for good cause (failure to meet qualifications, proposed transferee is competitor, refusal to comply, unpaid amounts)
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's discretion to block transfers

overrides.override_summary: Cannot prohibit franchisee from joining association of franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee organizing rights

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination with 60 days to cure; 180 days' notice for non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Much longer cure period than standard 24h-30d cure windows in FA; major franchisee protection

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota; cannot require jury trial waiver; cannot require consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Comprehensive override of dispute resolution provisions; protects Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/sale/transfer will not apply to claims under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves franchisee rights under Minnesota franchise law despite release requirement

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must indemnify Minnesota franchisees against third-party trademark infringement claims arising from franchisee's authorized use of marks
overrides.why_it_matters: Shifts trademark defense cost to franchisor

overrides.override_summary: Release will not apply to claims under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves ND franchise law claims despite release

overrides.override_summary: Covenants not to compete generally considered unenforceable in North Dakota; franchisor will seek to enforce to extent enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete effectively void in North Dakota — major franchisee benefit

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in North Dakota for claims under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta venue for ND franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: To the extent required by law, North Dakota law applies
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Georgia choice of law
